name: Salacia
description: Salacia is the Roman goddess of saltwater. She is often associated with the sea and is considered the female personification of the ocean's depths. Salacia is the wife of Neptune, the Roman god of the sea. She is depicted as a beautiful and powerful goddess, often riding in a chariot pulled by dolphins or seahorses, accompanied by a retinue of sea creatures. Her name is derived from the Latin word 'sal', meaning salt. She embodies the beauty, tranquility, and mystery of the ocean. As Neptune's queen, she ruled the seas alongside him, ensuring their bounty and safety for sailors and fishermen. While not as widely worshipped as other major Roman deities, Salacia held a significant place in Roman maritime culture and was invoked for safe voyages and prosperous fishing. She represents the hidden depths and unseen power of the ocean, a vital aspect of Roman life and trade. Salacia's influence can be seen in various Roman art and literature, where she is often depicted as a graceful and benevolent figure, a vital part of the Roman pantheon's control over the natural world, specifically the crucial role of the sea.
subtitle: Goddess of Saltwater and Neptune's Queen

keyMyths.title: Salacia's Marriage to Neptune
keyMyths.description: Neptune, captivated by Salacia's beauty, sought her hand in marriage. Initially hesitant, she hid from him, but Neptune eventually found her and convinced her to become his queen.

keyMyths.title: Rule of the Seas
keyMyths.description: As Neptune's queen, Salacia co-ruled the seas, ensuring their safety and abundance. She was responsible for the calm waters and the prosperity of those who sailed upon them.

keyMyths.title: The Hidden Depths
keyMyths.description: Salacia embodies the hidden depths and mysteries of the ocean. She represents the unseen power and beauty that lie beneath the waves, a realm both alluring and dangerous.

sources.author: Hyginus
sources.description: A collection of mythological stories, including the account of Salacia's marriage to Neptune.
sources.text: A collection of mythological stories, including the account of Salacia's marriage to Neptune.

sources.author: Various Roman Poets
sources.description: Mentions and allusions to Salacia in various poetic works, describing her role and attributes.
